content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA having NNN elements. Chef wants to make all the elements of the array equal by repeating the following move.


Choose any integer KKK between 111 and NNN (inclusive). Then choose KKK distinct indices i1,i2,…,iKi_1 , i_2, \dots, i_Ki1​,i2​,…,iK​, and increase the values of Ai1,Ai2,…,AiKA_{i_1} , A_{i_2} , \dots , A_{i_K}Ai1​​,Ai2​​,…,AiK​​ by 111.
Find the minimum number of moves required to make all the elements of the array equal.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer denoting the minimum number of moves required to make all the elements of the array equal.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum number of moves required to make all array elements equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: max=3, min=1, answer=2. Test case 2: all equal, answer=0.

hidden.label: edge case all equal large values

solution.approach: Since in one move we can increase any non‑empty subset of indices by 1, we can always increase all elements that are still below the current maximum. The final equal value cannot be smaller than the current maximum, so the optimal final value is max(A). Each element i needs (max(A) - Ai) increments, and we can give at most one increment to any element per move. Therefore the number of moves required is the largest of these required increments, i.e., max(A) - min(A). The answer for each test case is simply the difference between the maximum and minimum array values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case to find min and max, overall O(total N) ≤ O(10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides input storage.
